reviews.review_msg: Be prepared to fail. Teaches too fast. Doesn't explain clearly.

Jillian is a good prof. She cares about her students and is always accessible for extra help. The clicker questions helped me a lot with understanding the lecture materials. As long as you attend lectures /tutorials, do your homeworks, and keep up with the class you'll be fine!

Took a 300 level selected topics class with her on the philosophy of biology couple years ago. Demonstrates a genuine passion for philosophy. Fairly clear in her explanations and the syllabus was woven together in a progressive manner culminating in several contemporary discussions in the philosophy of evolutionary theory. Tough grader though.

I felt that Jillian did a fantastic job conducting a class on Phil of Sport although it was her first time. Undoubtedly, Jillian was a hard marker; however, she was also a fair marker who was willing to help her students understand course material. If you'd really like to benefit from taking a course with Jillian, be prepared give it your best.

Worst class I have ever taken. Enrolled  to fill out a requirement thinking a 100 level course would be simple. THIS WAS HARDER THAN 3rd YEAR CLASSES! Testing is complex and lectures are confusing. Do not take this class!

i am not native english speaker.my first time  of phil100 was D. then i retake. this time is Jill. i deep read all materials,  collorate with TAs who mark the papers. then i got A-. if u start to like the subject, u will get good mark. dont rely on course notes,but on readings, they r hard to understand but they give u high grades
Avoid this prof at all costs, GPA downer.. super curved class with failing averages.
no comment

The prof is a nice enough person, but I was very disappointed with her teaching. Provides partially filled pdfs that we you can take notes on, but she does not give enough time to take notes. Hardly writes, just talks. No tutorials to speak of. I think this course is just a cashcow because of BUS students. Avoid at all costs. I am very upset!!!

Worst class ever. She's  a terrible prof with bad taste in music. Avoid this class and her if you can. It is a nightmare and a torture just to withstand her lectures. Plus the midterm and final were so difficult that she had to scale the marks just so half the class wouldn't fail. Not to mention she ridicules the class if we don't understand.

DO NOT take her class!! She makes the class way more difficult than it needs to be.. her exams are really hard! She doesnt give enough examples in class, just goes over the concepts! She yelled at us during the exam/midterm for not staying in our seats at the end of the exam! Drastically lowered my CGPA

I came to hand in an assignment on the day that it was due.  She asked that we hand it in her box if we couldnt find her.  Just before class i caught up with her and said here is the assignment that is due today. She yelled at me for not handing it in her box even though She would be collecting that assignment 5 min from when i gave it to her.

she is the most terrible prof I have had in my life, she lowered my GPA so much that I could not transfer to the university that I wanted just because of her class grade. Useless and rude.definition of incomprehensible. When the average of class is 33 percent then there is something wrong with you or your course,not with the students.

Really difficult class if you're not a phil-minded student. TA's were the saving grace but even then not much could be done. Twisted multiple choice killed every exam mark (would have done much higher if not for that). And the little things mean alot when answering questions-so be careful. Not recommended as an "Easy Q" class because it's not!
Other things were okay but "Twisted" questions were terrible!!!! I kind of screwed this course...............Strongly recommend to get helps from TA

Her lectures are as simple and lame as the textbook. Phil really depends if u understand and can twist and play around with the concepts, but as a teacher, she doesn't help your understanding or reasoning to ideas. She basically use lame examples that undermine the course. Be prepared for her examples being about everyone failing the class...

philosophy, you either get it or u don't.Its diff for every person. The content that is studied and discussed just plays with your mind. It starts out easy but it just gets worse and worse. and the book doesnt help at all. jill is alright a lil weird but i guess shes okay at teaching the course. the course itself is crazy more than she is lol.

Test way to hard compared to the style of teaching. A fair amount of the class was about statistics (no math needed, just the concepts like correlation) which was surprising. Fairly easy material but you need to know it inside and out for the tests and this can be tricky.

This is a tough course to begin with but Prof Mc does the best she can to make it easier. It's important you go to class and ESPECIALLY tutorials (they help a lot). It might be tough to get a good mark...I think a C+ is the department avg and it's a very scaled class. Reading the textbook and doing the assignments helps too.
Material is not too hard but I find it hard to do well (like get As and Bs for raw score) simply because there is too little time to write the test.
Classes were fine; tests were much too difficult for material studied.

Notes are great to follow along, usually OK in the beginning then steers off like a bottle rocket and leaves everyone thinking wtf (granted, philosophy has that slight tendency). Thank god her curriculum somewhat parallels that of wikipedia articles. Tests have a tendency to include inference rules and sentential logic even if not touched in course
As long as you pay attention in class, you'll be fine. Midterm isn't too hard, and tutorials are very helpful. Show up to office hours, you'll get all the help you need.
she just doesnt know how to teach. Shes not organized, and about 85%  of the time she maks no sense.

took both phil 100 and xx1-was not a good prof. makes sarcastic jokes when you dont know the answer. she emails fill in the blank notes which are good but then they make you want to skip class. notes are often vague. essays are hard. if it was for bell curve, everyone would fail

Has fill in the blank notes that are extremely useful. Lectures are well organized. Philosophy itself can be pretty challenging but she is very fair. Didn't attend class often since it was early in the morning but still kept up. Recommended for an elective with short readings!

She knows her stuff and teaches well. Her notes are clear. The essay assignments are definitely tough but if you put the effort in you can do OK. The final exam was very hard but most probably she scaled it.

"Dr. Mac" supplies fill-in-the-blank style notes, which are pretty handy. She's good for a chat in her office. If you struggle with the basic concepts of Phil., you'll find her class challenging, but hopefully rewarding (you should be able to understand this material if you're in University!!!). Main con: sometimes lame humor, can be annoying.
She is just not a good person or a good prof. Too full of herself. Take this course with someone else and you will love it.
No Comments

Stay away from her. If you are planning to take this course with this person, consider: 1- not all phil teachers are this annoying - almost all are very nice. 2- not all phil courses are this much useless and random - most are very interesting and fun. 3- this course has as much to do with phil as it does with stats, medicine & everything else.
No Comments
she was a pretty good prof, but i hated the course to death. it's one of those courses where you get it or you don't (i didn't, obviously. but i passed).
No Comments
This course is HARD! Well, if you take it with MacIntosh...you will need to write 2 papers which worth a lot, no midterm though, but I have no idea what she is talking about.
